questions.question: Why is the Himalayan region considered geologically unstable?
questions.answer: Because it is a young fold mountain system formed by recent tectonic activity.

questions.question: Why are glaciers commonly found in the Himadri range?
questions.answer: Because it has very high altitude and remains snow-covered throughout the year.

questions.question: Why do rivers disappear in the Bhabar region?
questions.answer: Because the pebbles and rocks allow water to seep underground.

questions.question: Why is Khadar soil more fertile than Bhangar?
questions.answer: Because it is renewed every year by fresh river deposits.

questions.question: Why are the Northern Plains important for agriculture?
questions.answer: Because of fertile alluvial soil and abundant water.

questions.question: Why does the Deccan Plateau slope eastward?
questions.answer: Because its general relief gradually slopes towards the Bay of Bengal.

questions.question: Why are the Western Ghats continuous while Eastern Ghats are discontinuous?
questions.answer: Because rivers have cut through the Eastern Ghats creating gaps.

questions.question: Why do the Western Ghats receive heavy rainfall?
questions.answer: Because they block moisture-laden monsoon winds causing orographic rainfall.

questions.question: Why is vegetation sparse in the Indian Desert?
questions.answer: Because the region receives very low rainfall.

questions.question: Why do streams in the desert disappear into sand?
questions.answer: Because they do not have enough water to reach the sea.

questions.question: Why are the western coastal plains narrow?
questions.answer: Because the Western Ghats lie very close to the Arabian Sea.

questions.question: Why are eastern coastal plains wider than western ones?
questions.answer: Because large rivers form extensive deltas there.

questions.question: Why is Chilika Lake important?
questions.answer: It is the largest saltwater lake in India.

questions.question: Why are Lakshadweep islands called coral islands?
questions.answer: Because they are formed by coral deposits.

questions.question: Why are Andaman and Nicobar islands strategically important?
questions.answer: Because of their location in the Bay of Bengal near international sea routes.

questions.question: Why do the Himalayas influence India's climate?
questions.answer: They block cold winds from Central Asia.

questions.question: Why are the Northern Plains called the granary of India?
questions.answer: Because they produce large quantities of food grains.

questions.question: Why is the Peninsular Plateau important for industry?
questions.answer: Because it contains rich mineral resources.

questions.question: Why are rivers in the peninsula mostly seasonal compared to Himalayan rivers?
questions.answer: Because they depend mainly on rainfall.

questions.question: Why are sand dunes common in the Thar Desert?
questions.answer: Because strong winds move and deposit sand.

questions.question: Why do many ports develop along coastal plains?
questions.answer: Because they provide access to sea trade.

questions.question: Why do the Himalayas have deep valleys?
questions.answer: Because fast flowing rivers cut through the mountains.

questions.question: Why are plateaus often rich in minerals?
questions.answer: Because they consist of ancient crystalline rocks.

questions.question: Why are island groups important for biodiversity?
questions.answer: Because they contain diverse flora and fauna.

questions.question: Why are coastal regions important for fishing?
questions.answer: Because they have abundant marine resources.
